clinical_history: 58 year old woman with dyspnea. What is the most likely diagnosis based on this ECG?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a number of findings consistent with the diagnosis of very severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. The rhythm is borderline resting sinus tachycardia with atrial premature beats. The P waves are somewhat peaked (but not 2.5mm or more) with a rightward P wave axis (about +80 degrees) and there is frank right axis deviation of the QRS (close to +100 degrees).  Slow R wave progression is present and the precordial transition zone is delayed until at least V6. In addition, there are non-specific ST-T changes. Of interest, despite the severity of the COPD, the QRS voltage here is not absolutely low, but is relatively low overall.
